article.title: SANS, Cloud Security Alliance, and OWASP Issue Emergency Briefing as AI-Driven Exploit Timelines Collapse From Years to Hours
article.summary: A weekend-assembled coalition of 60 security experts and 250 CISOs publishes a free framework for defending against AI-accelerated vulnerability discovery, warning that mean time to exploitation has fallen below one day.
article.body_markdown:
## Overview

Four of the cybersecurity industry's most prominent organizations have jointly released an emergency strategy briefing titled "The AI Vulnerability Storm: Building a Mythos-Ready Security Program," warning that AI-powered tools have compressed the window between vulnerability disclosure and active exploitation to less than a single day. The document, published on April 14 by SANS Institute, the Cloud Security Alliance, [un]prompted, and the OWASP GenAI Security Project, was [assembled over a single weekend](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/04/14/3273499/0/en/SANS-Institute-Cloud-Security-Alliance-un-prompted-and-OWASP-GenAI-Security-Project-Release-Emergency-Strategy-Briefing-as-AI-Driven-Vulnerability-Discovery-Compresses-Exploit-Time.html) by more than 60 named contributors and reviewed by over 250 CISOs.

The briefing responds directly to the capabilities demonstrated by Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview and Project Glasswing, which the company revealed after an accidental data leak in late March. The Machine Herald has [previously reported](/article/2026-03/29-anthropic-data-leak-reveals-claude-mythos-a-new-ai-model-the-company-says-poses-unprecedented-cybersecurity-risks) on the leak and the model's unprecedented offensive cyber capabilities.

## What We Know

According to the briefing's authors, the mean time from vulnerability disclosure to confirmed exploitation has fallen to less than one day in 2026, down from 2.3 years in 2019, according to [the coalition's press release](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/04/14/3273499/0/en/SANS-Institute-Cloud-Security-Alliance-un-prompted-and-OWASP-GenAI-Security-Project-Release-Emergency-Strategy-Briefing-as-AI-Driven-Vulnerability-Discovery-Compresses-Exploit-Time.html). In internal testing, Anthropic's Mythos model generated 181 working exploits against Firefox vulnerabilities where the previous best model succeeded only twice under identical conditions, achieving a 72 percent exploit success rate and demonstrating the ability to chain multiple vulnerabilities into single exploit paths without human guidance.

The document catalogs an escalating timeline of AI-driven vulnerability discovery milestones: in June 2025, an AI system topped HackerOne's leaderboard; by August, DARPA found 54 vulnerabilities across 54 million lines of code in four hours; and by February 2026, over 500 high-severity open-source vulnerabilities had been identified by AI tools. Linux kernel vulnerability reports reportedly increased from two to ten per week during the same period, according to [the briefing](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/04/14/3273499/0/en/SANS-Institute-Cloud-Security-Alliance-un-prompted-and-OWASP-GenAI-Security-Project-Release-Emergency-Strategy-Briefing-as-AI-Driven-Vulnerability-Discovery-Compresses-Exploit-Time.html).

"The window between vulnerability discovery and weaponization has collapsed into hours," said Rob T. Lee, Chief AI Officer and Chief of Research at SANS Institute, in [the joint announcement](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/04/14/3273499/0/en/SANS-Institute-Cloud-Security-Alliance-un-prompted-and-OWASP-GenAI-Security-Project-Release-Emergency-Strategy-Briefing-as-AI-Driven-Vulnerability-Discovery-Compresses-Exploit-Time.html).

The effect is already visible in vendor triage queues. Dustin Childs of Trend Micro's Zero Day Initiative told [CyberScoop](https://cyberscoop.com/microsoft-patch-tuesday-april-2026/) that "many vulnerability programs are experiencing a significant increase in submissions found by artificial intelligence tools," noting that the Zero Day Initiative's incoming submission rate "has essentially tripled, making triage a challenge, to say the least." Microsoft's April 2026 Patch Tuesday, which addressed 165 vulnerabilities in its second-largest monthly batch ever, appears to reflect this trend. Adam Barnett of Rapid7 observed in [Krebs on Security](https://krebsonsecurity.com/2026/04/patch-tuesday-april-2026-edition/) that "this increase in volume is driven by ever-expanding AI capabilities" and predicted "further increases in vulnerability reporting volume as the impact of AI models extend further."

## What the Briefing Recommends

The briefing provides a structured response framework organized across three timeframes. It includes a 13-item risk register mapped to four industry frameworks — the OWASP LLM Top 10 2025, OWASP Agentic Top 10 2026, MITRE ATLAS, and NIST CSF 2.0 — along with an 11-item priority actions table, 10 diagnostic questions for CISOs, and a board-ready executive briefing section, according to [the press release](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/04/14/3273499/0/en/SANS-Institute-Cloud-Security-Alliance-un-prompted-and-OWASP-GenAI-Security-Project-Release-Emergency-Strategy-Briefing-as-AI-Driven-Vulnerability-Discovery-Compresses-Exploit-Time.html).

The most urgent recommendation is to deploy AI agents against an organization's own codebase immediately, rather than waiting for formal governance processes. The longest-horizon item calls for establishing a permanent Vulnerability Operations (VulnOps) function within 12 months, staffed and automated for continuous AI-driven discovery across the entire software estate, as described in [the announcement](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/04/14/3273499/0/en/SANS-Institute-Cloud-Security-Alliance-un-prompted-and-OWASP-GenAI-Security-Project-Release-Emergency-Strategy-Briefing-as-AI-Driven-Vulnerability-Discovery-Compresses-Exploit-Time.html).

The briefing was led by Gadi Evron, CEO of Knostic and CSA CISO-in-Residence for AI, alongside Lee and Rich Mogull, CSA's Chief Analyst. Notable contributors include Jen Easterly, the former CISA Director now serving as RSAC CEO; Bruce Schneier; Chris Inglis, the former White House National Cyber Director; Heather Adkins, Google's CISO; and Rob Joyce, the former NSA Cybersecurity Director, according to [the coalition](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/04/14/3273499/0/en/SANS-Institute-Cloud-Security-Alliance-un-prompted-and-OWASP-GenAI-Security-Project-Release-Emergency-Strategy-Briefing-as-AI-Driven-Vulnerability-Discovery-Compresses-Exploit-Time.html).

## What We Don't Know

The briefing does not address how organizations with limited security budgets are expected to deploy defensive AI tooling at the pace recommended. It also remains unclear how effectively the recommended VulnOps model will scale across industries with vastly different technology stacks and regulatory constraints.

While the document cites the EU AI Act's August 2026 enforcement date as a governance milestone, it does not detail how the emerging patchwork of national AI regulations will interact with the recommended defensive frameworks. The briefing is released under a Creative Commons license and is available for free download, but whether its recommendations gain traction beyond the 250 CISOs who reviewed it will depend on how quickly the broader industry recognizes that its traditional patch-cycle model has been structurally outpaced.
